latest.title: CEO Retirement
latest.content:
15 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 2774I
Luceco PLC
15 June 2026
15 June 2026
LUCECO PLC
CEO RETIREMENT
Luceco plc ("Luceco" or the "Group") announces that, following 21 years of leading the business, John Hornby has decided to retire from the Group, with effect from 31 December 2026.
John will be taking a sabbatical until late August 2026, and will remain as Chief Executive Officer and as a Board Director until 31 December, following which he will be available to the Group in an advisory capacity. During John's sabbatical, the Group's Senior Leadership Team, supported by the Board, will discharge the functions and responsibilities of the CEO.
The Board has commenced the process of identifying a permanent successor and will make a further announcement in due course.
Giles Brand, Chair of Luceco, said:
"On behalf of the Board, I would like to thank John for his longstanding contribution to Luceco. He has been an important part of Luceco's development, expanding the Group significantly into the successful business it is today, with a proven platform for growth. The Board and Luceco colleagues wish John the very best for the future."
John Hornby, commented:
"It has been a privilege to serve as Chief Executive Officer of Luceco and I am enormously proud of the Group's development over this period. With a strong platform for growth, underpinned by Luceco's competitive advantages and structural opportunities, the Group is confident in the prospects for the current financial year and beyond."
The trading momentum described in the Group's Q1 Trading Update published on 19 May 2026 has been sustained through Q2-to-date. The outlook for the year ending December 2026 remains consistent with the commentary provided in that announcement and accordingly, the Board continues to expect Adjusted Operating Profit to exceed £40m, with the potential for further significant outperformance dependent on Demand Flexibility. A half year trading update will be announced towards the end of July 2026.
Details of John Hornby's remuneration arrangements in connection with his retirement will be set out in the Section 430(2B) disclosure that will be made available on Luceco's website in due course, and will also be set out in the Remuneration Report.
Luceco plc
Contact
Giles Brand, Chair
(Via Sodali & Co)
Will Hoy, Chief Financial Officer
Sodali & Co
Contact
James White
Pete Lambie
Tilly Abraham
078 5543 2699 / 079 3535 1934
Note to Editors
Luceco plc (LSE:LUCE) is a leading designer and manufacturer of residential and commercial electrification products and systems. The Group designs and manufactures its market-leading range of wiring accessories, EV chargers, LED lighting, and portable power products at its state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities, distributing them through professional, wholesale and retail channels.
For more information, please visit
www.lucecoplc.com
.

company_30s.what_they_do: Luceco builds and sells electric‑vehicle charging stations and related infrastructure, plus software to manage the network. The product line covers fast chargers for private homes, commercial sites and fleet depots.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global push to electrify transport is creating a huge market for charging points. Luceco’s technology is positioned in the fast‑charging segment, which is critical for long‑range EVs, and its Chinese manufacturing base gives it a cost advantage over many peers.
company_30s.current_state: Luceco has posted solid quarterly earnings, reporting a 11 % organic revenue lift to about £68 m in Q1 2026. The company is expanding its EV‑charging portfolio and has recently announced a dividend of 4.2 p. The share price, however, is trading far below its all‑time high, reflecting a market that has sold off heavily despite the underlying growth.

rns_narrative: The company’s recent filings show a flurry of corporate‑governance activity: new directors, changes in director responsibilities, and a disclosure of a sizeable shareholding by a senior manager. These moves suggest the board is tightening control and signalling confidence.

Financially, Luceco delivered a strong Q1 update and announced a dividend. The AGM and Q1 results reinforce that the business is generating cash and growing revenue, which should calm sceptical investors.

The only negative driver is the lack of a clear catalyst beyond the usual quarterly cycle, but the dead Google Trends signal and the absence of widespread media coverage mean the market is still in a holding pattern.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism around solid earnings and a dividend push, but no real buzz.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls point to the Q1 revenue growth, the 4.2 p dividend and the company’s strategic position in fast‑charging tech. They view the current price as a bargain relative to the company’s fundamentals.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears flag the heavy sell‑off and the fact that the share has not yet shown any sign of stabilising. They worry that the company could still be in a volatility trap if a new regulatory hurdle or a supply‑chain issue appears.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has moved from neutral to mildly bullish after the Q1 update, but the overall conversation remains thin.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for ‘Luceco’ is flat, indicating the public is largely unaware of the stock’s current plight.

near_term_catalysts: Q2 2026 earnings – a stronger-than‑expected profit margin could trigger a rally.
Announcement of a new large‑scale charging contract in the UK or EU, which would demonstrate market traction.
Any regulatory announcement that eases EV‑charging infrastructure costs or provides subsidies.
A surge in trading volume that breaks out of the 52‑week low, signalling renewed interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike of at least 20 % above the 20‑day average coupled with a price break above the recent swing high. A positive earnings surprise or a new contract announcement would also validate the thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The price is still in the early recovery phase after a deep capitulation. The APEX compression score of 9/21 suggests the market is tightening, so a breakout is likely if the above catalysts materialise. If the price remains flat for several weeks, it could signal a missed opportunity.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The share is down 86 % from its peak – a classic deep‑crash scenario. Historical RED signals (average 162 % upside, 57 % win rate) show that such crashes often deliver huge rebounds. If the price recovers to 50 % of its all‑time high, that’s roughly a 100 % gain on the current level. Coupled with solid earnings and a dividend, the mean‑reversion thesis is strong.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that the market may stay in a volatility trap. If the company fails to secure new contracts or faces a regulatory setback, the price could keep sliding or stall. The worst realistic scenario is a 50 % further drop before any rebound.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk: 100 % of current price (if the stock falls to zero). Reward: up to 200 % if the price doubles to 2× current level. The asymmetry is roughly 1:2, which is attractive for a contrarian play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above the 52‑week low with volume >10 k shares and a 5‑day moving‑average confirmation. Use a 5 % stop below the entry. Size the position to 10 % of your discretionary capital, scaling up if the price confirms a trend.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until Q2 earnings or a new contract is announced. If volume spikes 20 % above the 20‑day average and the price touches the recent swing high, that is the trigger to enter.
playbook.if_passing: No genuine terminal risks exist. The only scenario that would force a sell is a bankruptcy filing or delisting notice, which are not currently indicated.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep 86 % crash, strong earnings momentum and a silent market create a high‑reward contrarian play – if the price rebounds to double its current level, you could double your stake.
